name: Cranberry & Raisin Chutney
recipeIngredient: 2 small Pears, peeled, cored and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a heavy large saucepan.
2. Add raisins. Remove from heat and let stand for 15 minutes. Drain, reserving half the liquid.
3. Pour the reserved raisin liquid into the same saucepan. Add sugar and white wine vinegar. Stir over medium-low heat until the sugar dissolves.
4. Increase the heat and bring to a boil without stirring, until the syrup turns golden brown. Brush down the sides of the pan with a wet pastry brush.
5. Remove from heat. Mix in orange juice, orange peel, and fresh ginger. Add cranberries and cook until they pop, about 5 minutes.
6. Stir in raisins, chopped pears, and toasted almonds. Cook for 1 minute.
7. Cover and refrigerate the chutney until well chilled. The chutney can be made up to 2 days in advance and kept refrigerated.
